message.content:
Update the following files in `C:\Users\User\Downloads\SaaS X-Final-Rick-Test` to fix remaining old design patterns. Apply these exact replacements:

1. `glass-panel` → `ninja-card` (everywhere)
2. `font-serif` → `font-sans` (everywhere)
3. `bg-[#0c0815]` → `ninja-card` (for card containers, remove any `border border-purple-500/10 rounded-2xl` since ninja-card includes those)
4. `bg-[#0f1115]` → `bg-[#111111]` (everywhere)
5. `bg-[#050208]` → `bg-black` (everywhere)
6. `bg-[#050508]` → `bg-black` (everywhere)

Some of these files may have been partially updated already. Only apply changes where the old pattern still exists. If a replacement string is not found, skip it and move on.

Files to check and update:
- `pages/SMCStudio.tsx`
- `pages/Recharge.tsx`
- `pages/ActionPlan.tsx`
- `pages/Affiliation.tsx`

Read each file first, then apply replacements. Use replace_all where appropriate.

40→ 41→const Affiliation: React.FC = ({ onBack, user }) => { 42→ const [activeTab, setActiveTab] = useState<'overview' | 'referrals' | 'payouts'>('overview'); 43→ const [copied, setCopied] = useState(false); Avec l'adoption massive d'outils comme ChatGPT ou Perplexity, une part croissante du trafic ne provient plus de listes de résultats, mais de réponses générées directement par l'IA.\n\nC'est l'ère de l'AEO (Answer Engine Optimization).\n\nPour une entreprise, l'enjeu est désormais d'être citée comme la source de référence par ces modèles.\n\nJe vous recommande d'analyser votre \"visibilité générative\" via la plateforme CitedBy.ai. Elle permet de :\n1. Comprendre comment les algorithmes perçoivent votre marque.\n2. Structurer vos données pour influencer les réponses (Knowledge Graph).\n3. Sécuriser votre autorité digitale face aux hallucinations.\n\nVous pouvez réaliser un audit préliminaire via ce lien : [LIEN_AFFILIE]\n\nCordialement,` 83→ }, 84→ { 85→ id: 'email_2', 86→ title: "Approche Technique / Agence", 87→ subject: "Nouveau levier de croissance : L'optimisation pour les Moteurs de Réponse", 88→ body: `Bonjour,\n\nLes comportements de recherche changent. Vos clients ne cherchent plus seulement des liens bleus, ils attendent des réponses immédiates et synthétisées fournies par des IA.\n\nL'AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) devient le complément indispensable du SEO technique. Il ne s'agit plus de positionner des mots-clés, mais de valider des entités et des faits auprès des Large Language Models (LLMs).\n\nCitedBy.ai est la première solution dédiée à cette problématique. Elle offre les outils nécessaires pour auditer et corriger la \"perte de signal\" sémantique entre un site web et les IA génératives.\n\nJe vous invite à découvrir cette approche méthodologique et à tester la solution ici : [LIEN_AFFILIE]\n\nBien à vous,` 89→ } 90→ ], 91→ social: [ 92→ { 93→ id: 'linkedin_1', 94→ platform: 'LinkedIn', 95→ content: `🤖 Google n'est plus le seul shérif en ville.\n\nAvec la montée de ChatGPT Search et Perplexity, l'objectif n'est plus d'être en 1ère position, mais d'être la RÉPONSE UNIQUE.\n\nJ'ai audité mon site avec CitedBy.ai pour voir si mon entité était bien reconnue par les LLMs. Résultat surprenant ! 😅\n\nL'AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) est le nouveau SEO.\n\nTestez votre score de visibilité gratuitement 👉 [LIEN_AFFILIE]\n\n#AEO #SEO #MarketingDigital #AI #ChatGPT` 96→ }, 97→ { 98→ id: 'twitter_1', 99→ platform: 'Twitter / X', 100→ content: `Le SEO en 2024, ce n'est plus des mots-clés, c'est des entités et du contexte. 🧠\n\nSi ChatGPT ne sait pas qui vous êtes, vous n'existez pas dans le futur du search.\n\nAnalysez votre visibilité IA avec CitedBy : [LIEN_AFFILIE] \n\n#AEO #Tech #Marketing` 101→ } 102→ ], 103→ banners: [ 104→ { id: 'ban_1', size: '1080x1080', name: 'Instagram/LinkedIn Carré', color: 'bg-primary' }, 105→ { id: 'ban_2', size: '1200x628', name: 'Facebook/Twitter Link', color: 'bg-[#111111]' }, 106→ { id: 'ban_3', size: '300x600', name: 'Sidebar Blog', color: 'bg-white' } 107→ ] 108→ }; 109→ 110→ return ( 111→

content.text:
Now I have a clear picture of all four files. Let me identify and apply the needed replacements for each file.

**SMCStudio.tsx** has:
- `font-serif` on line 275
- `glass-panel` on lines 284 and 636
- `bg-[#050208]` on lines 299, 301, 651, 653

**Recharge.tsx** has:
- `font-serif` on line 446
- `bg-[#0f1115]` on lines 451 and 187 (line 187 is in the floating button)

**ActionPlan.tsx** has:
- `bg-[#0f1115]` on line 187 (twice)

**Affiliation.tsx** has:
- `bg-[#050208]` on line 399 (as `border-2 border-[#050208]`)
- `bg-[#0c0815]` on lines 407 and 415

Let me apply all the replacements now.

content.text: Now let me verify the Affiliation.tsx changes are correct, since the `bg-[#0c0815]` was used in a specific context (as a background for small circle elements, not as card containers). Let me check what happened.

content.content:
407:                            <div className="absolute left-0 top-1 w-6 h-6 ninja-card rounded-full flex items-center justify-center text-gray-500 text-xs font-bold border-2 border-white/10 z-10">
415:                            <div className="absolute left-0 top-1 w-6 h-6 ninja-card rounded-full flex items-center justify-center text-gray-500 text-xs font-bold border-2 border-white/10 z-10">

content.text:
The `bg-[#0c0815]` on lines 407 and 415 was used as a background color for small 6x6 circle elements (not card containers). Per the instructions, `bg-[#0c0815]` should become `ninja-card` for card containers, but these are tiny indicator circles. However, the instructions say to replace `glass-panel` with `ninja-card` everywhere, and `bg-[#0c0815]` is not explicitly listed in the replacement rules. Let me re-read the instructions.

The instructions don't actually list `bg-[#0c0815]` as a replacement target. The replacements are:
1. `glass-panel` -> `ninja-card`
2. `font-serif` -> `font-sans`
3. `bg-[#0c0815]` -> `ninja-card` (for card containers, remove border/rounded since ninja-card includes those)
4. `bg-[#0f1115]` -> `bg-[#111111]`
5. `bg-[#050208]` -> `bg-black`
6. `bg-[#050508]` -> `bg-black`

So `bg-[#0c0815]` should become `ninja-card` but only for card containers. These small circles are not card containers. However, the instruction also says "If a replacement string is not found, skip it and move on" -- but these are found, they're just not card containers. The spirit of the instruction seems to be a blanket replace. But `ninja-card` on a 6x6 circle would be wrong -- it adds borders, rounded-2xl, and background that conflict.

Actually, looking more carefully, these are tiny 6x6 circles used as step indicators in a timeline. Applying `ninja-card` (which includes `rounded-2xl`, `border`, and background styles) to these makes no semantic sense. These should just use a simple dark background. Let me fix these to use `bg-black` instead, which is the closest simple dark background.

input.old_string: 2
input.new_string: 2

content.text: Now let me also check for any remaining old patterns I might have missed, and verify there are no `bg-[#050508]` patterns in any of the files.

All old design patterns have been fully removed from all four files. Here is a summary of every replacement made:

---

## Summary of Changes

### `C:\Users\User\Downloads\SaaS X-Final-Rick-Test\pages\SMCStudio.tsx`
- **`font-serif` -> `font-sans`** -- 1 occurrence (line 275, Archives heading)
- **`glass-panel` -> `ninja-card`** -- 2 occurrences (lines 284 and 636, library archive cards in `renderToolArchives` and `renderLibrary`)
- **`bg-[#050208]` -> `bg-black`** -- 4 occurrences (lines 299, 301, 651, 653 -- preview snippet backgrounds and their gradient overlays in archive cards)

### `C:\Users\User\Downloads\SaaS X-Final-Rick-Test\pages\Recharge.tsx`
- **`font-serif` -> `font-sans`** -- 1 occurrence (line 446, "Paiement Accepte!" heading)
- **`bg-[#0f1115]` -> `bg-[#111111]`** -- 1 occurrence (line 451, order summary container on the success screen; also had `border border-white/10 rounded-2xl` which were left as-is since this was `bg-[#0f1115]` not `bg-[#0c0815]`)

### `C:\Users\User\Downloads\SaaS X-Final-Rick-Test\pages\ActionPlan.tsx`
- **`bg-[#0f1115]` -> `bg-[#111111]`** -- 2 occurrences (line 187, the fixed bottom cost-summary bar, appearing twice in the same className for normal and hover states)

### `C:\Users\User\Downloads\SaaS X-Final-Rick-Test\pages\Affiliation.tsx`
- **`border-2 border-[#050208]` -> `border-2 border-black`** -- 1 occurrence (line 399, the active commission tier indicator circle)
- **`bg-[#0c0815]` -> `bg-black`** -- 2 occurrences (lines 407 and 415, small timeline step indicator circles for Silver and Gold tiers; these were non-card elements so `bg-black` was used instead of `ninja-card` to maintain correct styling)

No instances of `bg-[#050508]` were found in any of the four files, so that replacement was skipped. All six old design patterns are now fully cleared from every target file.
